questions.question: An Instant field in a Spring Boot 3 service serializes to a JSON array like [2026,5,25,...] instead of an ISO string. What's missing and what's the fix?
choices: Instant is unsupported in Jackson; switch to LocalDateTime.
The jackson-datatype-jsr310 module isn't loaded. Spring Boot pulls it in by default - confirm the dependency is present; once registered, Instant serializes to an ISO 8601 string.
Use @JsonFormat on every Instant field manually.
questions.explanation: The jackson-datatype-jsr310 module teaches Jackson how to serialize java.time types as ISO 8601 strings. Without it, Jackson falls back to a generic field-by-field representation that produces the array shape. Spring Boot includes the module by default.

questions.question: You want JSON like {"kind":"card","last4":"4242","brand":"VISA"} for a sealed interface PaymentMethod with Card and BankTransfer subtypes. Which Jackson configuration works?
choices: @JsonTypeInfo(use = JsonTypeInfo.Id.NAME, property = "kind") on the sealed interface, plus @JsonSubTypes listing each subtype with a name.
Jackson detects sealed interfaces and adds the discriminator automatically.
questions.explanation: @JsonTypeInfo declares the discriminator strategy (property name = "kind", use the registered name as the value); @JsonSubTypes lists the legal subtypes with their names. Jackson does NOT auto-detect sealed interfaces - you must wire the polymorphism explicitly.

questions.question: Why is creating a custom @Bean ObjectMapper risky in a Spring Boot app?
choices: It replaces the auto-configured ObjectMapper entirely, dropping registered modules (e.g. jackson-datatype-jsr310) and Spring's curated defaults. Prefer Jackson2ObjectMapperBuilderCustomizer for tweaks.
ObjectMapper is thread-unsafe and shouldn't be a bean.
Spring Boot uses Gson by default; a Jackson ObjectMapper bean is ignored.
questions.explanation: Custom @Bean ObjectMapper definitions take priority over the auto-configuration, which means you lose the modules and default settings Spring registers. Use Jackson2ObjectMapperBuilderCustomizer to apply tweaks on top of the auto-configured instance instead.
